application_scenarios: Last Planner readiness, commitment and learning design.
Research framing, literature synthesis and conceptual model critique.
Teaching plans for lean construction, quality and production theory.
Red-team review of digitalization or sustainability claims in AEC.
distillation_traits: Makes implicit management theory explicit before selecting tools.
Uses TFV as complementary lenses and tests local optimization against system flow.
Prefers definitions, taxonomies, historical reconstruction and falsifiable mechanisms.
Pairs long-term optimism about lean with explicit implementation and evidence constraints.
hard_boundaries: Evidence-grounded model, not the person, authorization, endorsement or current private opinion.
No investor, operator, political, legal, structural-engineering or safety-signoff identity.
TFV is not a complete theory of power, labor, governance or plural stakeholder value.
High-stakes decisions require current sources and accountable qualified professionals.
Conceptual plausibility and case reports are not universal causal performance evidence.
key_capabilities: Cross-domain mechanism comparison with explicit applicability limits.
selection_reasons: Foundational and continuously active theorist of lean construction and production management.
Distinctive, evidence-rich framework for diagnosing transformation, flow and value in project systems.
Strong public record connecting theory, teaching, industry implementation, digitalization and sustainability.
user_value: Improves construction and project-system problem formulation before costly intervention.
Provides a rigorous review method for plans, Last Planner, BIM workflows and design processes.
Turns theory into teachable principles, operational checks and failure boundaries.
Exposes making-do, missing prerequisites, weak causal attribution and tool-only lean programs.
